## Four-Zone Model (3D)

This section adds a four-zone ogive/cylinder/boattail/base decomposition, an
AoF-resolved ground projection, and an angle-dependent presented-area geometry
factor. It does **not** retire the single-belt treatment above: the two are
kept as **coexisting field models with a division of labor** (below), computed
side by side.

### Three field variants — naming and division of labor

The model carries three distinct spatial treatments of the fragment field.
They are easy to blur together, so this table fixes the names used throughout:

| Variant | What it is | Governing eqs | Symmetry / output | Kept for |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1-D symmetric disk** | scalar lethality vs. a single radius $r$ at burst height | (9)–(10) | azimuthally uniform ring; $N_\text{eff}(r)$, $P_\text{kill}(r)$, $R_{50}$ | the calibrated absolute-lethality anchor ($R_{50}$ vs. TM 9-1901); simplest reading |
| **Single-zone (equatorial-belt) 3-D field** | one cylinder belt projected onto the ground with AoF + height of burst | (9) kernel + (15)–(17) projection | azimuthally symmetric footprint on $(x,y,z)$ | drawing-data-free baseline (needs only bulk mass/caliber/wall); the reference the four-zone diff is measured against |
| **Four-zone 3-D field** | four belts (ogive/cyl/boattail/base), each with its own $V_0^z$, $\mu^z$, $\theta^z$ | (11)–(14), (22′) | forward/rear **lobed** footprint, AoF-resolved | directional structure — the fore/aft lobes the single belt cannot produce |

**Why both 3-D variants are live, not old-vs-new.** The single-zone belt is the
azimuthally-symmetric baseline; the four-zone adds the fore/aft lobes from the
end zones. The sensitivity app computes **both on the same grid**, so their
cell-by-cell difference is **grid-exact** — no interpolation or resampling error
enters it. That difference is a useful *indicator* of where the end zones add or
remove lethality, but it is **not a clean physical isolation** of the
ogive/base/boattail contribution. The single-zone baseline is the *legacy* belt
path (`_expected_kills_3d_point`), which uses a backward shell-axis convention
($\hat{e} = (-\cos\alpha, 0, -\sin\alpha)$) and a different off-boresight
normalization than the four-zone path's forward axis
($(+\cos\alpha, 0, -\sin\alpha)$). The two coincide *by construction* only on the
equatorial belt / boresight plane; off it, the diff conflates the genuine
end-zone signal with a residual axis-convention and normalization mismatch that
has not been reconciled (§12; belt-axis note in
`updates/lethal-fragment-density-field/derivation.md` §5.4). Read the diff as
directional structure, not as an exact per-zone attribution. That diff only
exists because both are retained. They also differ in inputs and
validation status: the single-zone belt needs only bulk shell parameters and
carries the calibrated absolute magnitude, whereas the four-zone needs the
drawing-derived registry geometry (CRH, per-zone lengths) and its **absolute**
$R_{50}$ is pending recalibration (§12) — only its *relative* zone contributions
and lobed structure are trustworthy in absolute terms. So the single-zone belt
is the drawing-free, absolute-magnitude reference; the four-zone is the
directional-structure model. Neither supersedes the other.

References:

- `updates/frag-field-3d-geometry/derivation.md` — four-zone derivation (PASS).
- `updates/target-area-profile/derivation.md` — $A_p(\gamma, \text{posture})$ derivation (PASS).

### Notation note — Mott constant rename

The Mott statistical fragmentation parameter (named `gamma` in
`SteelParams`) collides notationally with $\gamma$, the fragment **arrival
elevation angle** from horizontal used throughout the 3D geometry. To
disambiguate, this section uses **$\gamma_M$** (a.k.a. `gamma_M` in code)
for the Mott material constant and reserves **$\gamma$** for the arrival
angle.

### 6.1 · Zone geometry definitions

Four zones with axial extents $L_n$ (ogive), $L_c$ (cylinder), $L_t$
(boattail) and a base plate of thickness $t_b$. Tier-1 shells carry full
drawing-derived arc radii; Tier-2 shells fall back to fixed fractions
(scoping §3.2).

Per-zone Gurney velocity uses zone-local $M^z/C^z$:

$$
V_0^z = k^z \cdot \frac{V_g}{\sqrt{M^z/C^z + 1/2}} \quad (11)
$$

where $V_g = \sqrt{2E}$ is the explosive's Gurney constant and $k^z$ is a
zone reduction factor (default 1.0; base plate uses $k^b = 0.75$ for M1
and $k^b = 0.70$ for M107 per NWC TP 7124 rarefaction). $C^z$ is the zone
explosive allocation, distributed by interior bore volume; the base plate
has no interior cavity and is driven by an equivalent column
$C^b_\text{eff} = C^c \cdot (t_b / L_c)$ (acknowledged approximation,
§3.3).

Zone Mott half-mass uses zone-local $r_{bu}^z$ and $V_0^z$ (Gold 2017,
eq. 16):

$$
\mu^z = \sqrt{\frac{2}{\rho_s}}\left(\frac{\sigma_F}{\gamma_M}\right)^{3/2}\!\left(\frac{r_{bu}^z}{V_0^z}\right)^{3} \quad (12)
$$

**Unit check for eq. (12)** (added per reviewer deferred item):
$\sqrt{\text{m}^3/\text{kg}} \cdot \text{Pa}^{3/2} \cdot (\text{m/(m/s)})^3$
$= (\text{m}^{3/2} \text{kg}^{-1/2}) \cdot (\text{kg/(m·s}^2))^{3/2}
\cdot \text{s}^3$
$= \text{m}^{3/2-3/2} \cdot \text{kg}^{-1/2+3/2} \cdot \text{s}^{-3+3} = \text{kg}$
✓.

Per-zone spray elevation $\theta^z$ from forward shell axis:

| Zone | $\theta^z$ |
|---|---|
| Cylinder | $90°$ (exact, hoop symmetry) |
| Boattail | $90° + \tfrac{1}{2}\,\theta_{bt}^\text{full}$ |
| Base | $165°$ (phenomenological) |
| Ogive (Tier-1) | $90° - \arctan\!\bigl((r_o(L_n/2)-r_c^o)/(L_n/2 - x_c^o)\bigr)$ |
| Ogive (Tier-2) | $90° - \arcsin\!\bigl(\sqrt{\text{CRH}-1/4}/(2\,\text{CRH})\bigr)$ |

(The `boattail_angle_deg` field is the **full** included taper angle from
the drawing; the spray offset is half of that.)

### 6.2 · Zone computation

The four-zone decomposition lives in `src/arty/zones.py` —
`compute_shell_zones`, `fragment_ground_impact`, and `four_zone_field`,
backed by the drawing-derived registry in `arty.shells`.

```{python}
from arty.fragmentation import STANDING, PRONE, presented_area, retardation_coeff
from arty.zones import (
    compute_shell_zones, fragment_ground_impact, four_zone_field,
    ZoneParams, ShellZones, CRH_DEFAULT_TIER2,
)
from arty.shells import SHELLS
```

### 6.3 · Zone breakdown for 105 mm M1 HE

```{python}
zones_m1 = compute_shell_zones(SHELLS["105mm M1 HE"])
M_steel_m1 = (
    SHELLS["105mm M1 HE"].mass_total
    - SHELLS["105mm M1 HE"].mass_filler
    - SHELLS["105mm M1 HE"].mass_deductions
)

print("=== 105 mm M1 HE — Four-Zone Breakdown ===")
print(f"  Total steel mass : {M_steel_m1:.2f} kg")
print(f"{'Zone':<10}{'M_z (kg)':>10}{'frac':>8}{'C_z (kg)':>10}"
      f"{'M/C':>8}{'V0 (m/s)':>10}{'μ (g)':>8}{'spray°':>9}")
for name, z in [("Ogive", zones_m1.ogive), ("Cyl", zones_m1.cylinder),
                ("Boattail", zones_m1.boattail), ("Base", zones_m1.base)]:
    mc = z.mass_kg / z.C_kg if z.C_kg > 0 else float("nan")
    mu_g = z.mu * 1e3 if np.isfinite(z.mu) else float("nan")
    print(f"  {name:<8}{z.mass_kg:>10.3f}{z.mass_kg/M_steel_m1:>8.2%}"
          f"{z.C_kg:>10.3f}{mc:>8.2f}{z.V0_ms:>10.0f}{mu_g:>8.2f}"
          f"{z.spray_deg:>9.1f}")

# Spec assertions (zone-model spec)
assert 0.25 < zones_m1.ogive.mass_kg / M_steel_m1 < 0.42, "M1 ogive fraction out of spec"
assert 0.35 < zones_m1.cylinder.mass_kg / M_steel_m1 < 0.55, "M1 cylinder fraction out of spec"
assert 75.0 < zones_m1.ogive.spray_deg < 88.0, "M1 ogive spray angle out of spec"
print("\n  ✓ ogive ∈ [25 %, 42 %], cylinder ∈ [35 %, 55 %], spray ∈ [75°, 88°]")
```

### 6.4 · Zone breakdown for 155 mm M107 HE and 75 mm M48 HE

```{python}
zones_m107 = compute_shell_zones(SHELLS["155mm M107 HE"])
M_steel_m107 = (
    SHELLS["155mm M107 HE"].mass_total
    - SHELLS["155mm M107 HE"].mass_filler
    - SHELLS["155mm M107 HE"].mass_deductions
)
print("=== 155 mm M107 HE — Four-Zone Breakdown ===")
for name, z in [("Ogive", zones_m107.ogive), ("Cyl", zones_m107.cylinder),
                ("Boattail", zones_m107.boattail), ("Base", zones_m107.base)]:
    mc = z.mass_kg / z.C_kg if z.C_kg > 0 else float("nan")
    print(f"  {name:<8}  M={z.mass_kg:6.2f} kg ({z.mass_kg/M_steel_m107:.0%})"
          f"  V0={z.V0_ms:.0f} m/s  spray={z.spray_deg:.1f}°")
assert 75.0 < zones_m107.ogive.spray_deg < 88.0, "M107 ogive spray out of spec"

print("\n=== 75 mm M48 HE (Tier-2) — Four-Zone Breakdown ===")
zones_m48 = compute_shell_zones(SHELLS["75mm M48 HE"])
M_steel_m48 = (
    SHELLS["75mm M48 HE"].mass_total
    - SHELLS["75mm M48 HE"].mass_filler
    - SHELLS["75mm M48 HE"].mass_deductions
)
for name, z in [("Ogive", zones_m48.ogive), ("Cyl", zones_m48.cylinder),
                ("Boattail", zones_m48.boattail), ("Base", zones_m48.base)]:
    print(f"  {name:<8}  M={z.mass_kg:5.2f} kg ({z.mass_kg/M_steel_m48:.0%})"
          f"  V0={z.V0_ms:.0f} m/s  spray={z.spray_deg:.1f}°")
```

### 6.5 · AoF ground projection

A fragment leaving zone $z$ with polar angle $\theta^z$ from the forward
shell axis and azimuth $\phi$ around the axis has shell-frame direction
$\hat{v}_\text{shell} = (\cos\theta^z, \sin\theta^z\cos\phi, \sin\theta^z\sin\phi)$.
Rotation to the ground frame by $R_y(\text{AoF})$:

$$
v_{g,x} = \cos(\text{AoF})\cos\theta^z + \sin(\text{AoF})\sin\theta^z\sin\phi \quad (13a)
$$
$$
v_{g,y} = \sin\theta^z\cos\phi \quad (13b)
$$
$$
v_{g,z} = -\sin(\text{AoF})\cos\theta^z + \cos(\text{AoF})\sin\theta^z\sin\phi \quad (13c)
$$

Ground impact (burst at height $h_b$):

$$
x_\text{hit} = -\frac{v_{g,x}}{v_{g,z}}\,h_b, \qquad y_\text{hit} = -\frac{v_{g,y}}{v_{g,z}}\,h_b,
\qquad \sin\gamma = |v_{g,z}| \quad (14)
$$

valid when $v_{g,z} < 0$. Fragments with $v_{g,z} \ge 0$ travel upward or
horizontally and do not reach the ground in the straight-line model
(guarded explicitly). The projection is implemented as
`arty.zones.fragment_ground_impact`.

```{python}
# Spot-check: AoF=90° (vertical shell) → ogive fragments hit ring of
# radius h_b * tan(theta_o) (limit check from derivation §5)
h_b_test = 5.0
theta_o_test = zones_m1.ogive.spray_deg
hits = []
for phi in np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 9, endpoint=False):
    res = fragment_ground_impact(theta_o_test, phi, aof_deg=90.0, h_b=h_b_test)
    if res is not None:
        hits.append(res)
r_ring_obs = np.array([np.hypot(x, y) for x, y, _ in hits])
r_ring_pred = h_b_test * np.tan(np.radians(theta_o_test))
print(f"AoF = 90° vertical-shell ogive ring check:")
print(f"  predicted r = h_b tan(θ_o) = {r_ring_pred:.2f} m")
print(f"  measured r  = {r_ring_obs.mean():.2f} m (std {r_ring_obs.std():.2e})")
assert np.allclose(r_ring_obs, r_ring_pred, atol=1e-6), "circular symmetry broken"
print("  ✓ azimuthally symmetric at AoF = 90°")
```

### 6.6 · Presented-area-based geometry factor

The width-based factor $w/(2\pi s)$ in eq. (9) is replaced by the
$A_p$-based factor from `target-area-profile/derivation.md` eq. (P4):

$$
g_\text{new} = \frac{A_p(\gamma,\text{posture})}{2\pi\,s^2\,\cdot\,2\sin\theta^z\,\delta},
\qquad
A_p(\gamma,\text{posture}) = w_\perp\bigl(h\cos\gamma + d\sin\gamma\bigr) \quad (22')
$$

The geometry factor now falls off as $s^{-2}$ rather than $s^{-1}$ — the
correct inverse-square law for an isotropic point source. (See
target-area-profile derivation §4.3 for why this is not a clean limit of
the old eq. 22; absolute hit counts and the calibrated $R_{50}$ will need
to be re-anchored against TM 9-1901 — listed as a limitation.) The
$A_p(\gamma, \text{posture})$ function lives in `arty.fragmentation`.

```{python}
# Re-verify Ap(γ) limits
ap_stand_h = presented_area(0.0,           STANDING)   # horizontal fragment
ap_stand_v = presented_area(np.pi/2,       STANDING)   # vertical fragment
ap_prone_h = presented_area(0.0,           PRONE)
ap_prone_v = presented_area(np.pi/2,       PRONE)
print("Presented-area limits:")
print(f"  STANDING γ=0  : {ap_stand_h:.2f} m²   (expect 0.85)")
print(f"  STANDING γ=π/2: {ap_stand_v:.2f} m²   (expect 0.15)")
print(f"  PRONE    γ=0  : {ap_prone_h:.2f} m²   (expect 0.15)")
print(f"  PRONE    γ=π/2: {ap_prone_v:.2f} m²   (expect 0.90)")
assert abs(ap_stand_h - 0.85) < 0.01
assert abs(ap_prone_v - 0.90) < 0.01
print("  ✓ all four limits match derivation §3.8")
```

### 6.7 · Four-zone field and ground-impact diagnostic

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from arty import plots

# Compute drag-lambda grid once
n_mass = 200
m_grid_3d = np.logspace(-6, np.log10(0.5), n_mass)
lam_3d = retardation_coeff(m_grid_3d, drag, rho_steel)

# Field for M1, AoF = 30°, h_b = 2 m, STANDING target
X3d, Y3d, P3d = four_zone_field(
    zones_m1, aof_deg=30.0, h_b=2.0, posture=STANDING,
    drag_lam_grid=lam_3d, m_grid=m_grid_3d,
    max_r=60.0, n_grid=50,
)

fig = plots.fig_four_zone_field(X3d, Y3d, P3d)
fig.savefig("four_zone_field_m1.png", dpi=120, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

### 6.8 · Per-zone ground footprint (deterministic rays)

To visualise which zone hits where, sample the four zones with a fan of
azimuthal rays at AoF = 30° and plot their ground-impact points.

```{python}
fig = plots.fig_zone_footprint(zones_m1, aof_deg=30.0, h_b=2.0, n_phi=60)
fig.savefig("four_zone_rings.png", dpi=120, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

The forward sector (positive $x$) is dominated by ogive rays, the
equatorial belt by cylinder rays, and the rearward sector by base rays;
boattail rays cluster just behind the cylinder belt. This is the lobed
structure the single-zone equatorial-belt field is deliberately silent on —
the four-zone field's distinct contribution. This figure shows that structure
**directly**: it plots each zone's own ground-impact rays and performs **no
subtraction** against the belt baseline. It is not the belt-vs-four-zone cell
difference — that diff is a separate and only *approximate* indicator of where
the end zones add or remove lethality, and is **not** a clean per-zone
isolation (it conflates the genuine end-zone signal with an unreconciled
axis-convention/normalization mismatch; see "Why both 3-D variants are live"
above and §12).

### 6.9 · Burst geometry cross-section (x–z plane)

The elevation cross-section shows the $y = 0$ plane: shell arrival arrow,
burst point, and the two bounding rays per zone ($\phi = +90°$ solid,
$\phi = -90°$ dashed). Rays that reach the ground are drawn to their
impact point; rays with $v_{gz} \ge 0$ are drawn as dashed arrows
annotated "↑ excl." — they never enter the ground-impact model.

At $\text{AoF} = 90°$ the cylinder zone (spray angle $\theta_z = 90°$)
produces $v_{gz} = 0$ for both $\phi = \pm 90°$, so these horizontal rays are
excluded from this ground-impact *ray/dot* view (it requires $v_{gz} < 0$).
This is a limit of the ray renderer only: the graded four-zone $P_\text{kill}$
field itself (§6.7) no longer shows a near-burst "safe zone" at steep angles of
fall — as of v 0.6.0 its belt gate is evaluated at the height where the belt
illuminates the standing/prone target column, not on the $z = 0$ feet ray, so a
horizontal belt at burst height is scored lethal at chest/head height (see the
Change Log and the false-safe-zone note in §Limitations;
`updates/familyA-false-safe-zone/derivation.md`).

```{python}
fig = plots.fig_zone_elevation(zones_m1, aof_deg=30, h_b=2.0, r_person=30)
fig.savefig("four_zone_elevation.png", dpi=120, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

______________________________________________________________________
